stories.headline: Oil prices plunge as Iran says Strait 'open'
stories.summary: Brent crude sinks 10% after Iran says the key waterway is completely open for commercial ships for the rest of the ceasefire.

stories.headline: Iran Says Strait of Hormuz Is Completely Open for Commercial Ships
stories.summary: Iran says the Strait of Hormuz is completely open for commercial traffic, a major step toward ending the war with the US and Israel. Oil prices plunged on the news. Bloomberg's Tyler Kendall reports.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Dollar Wipes Out Iran War Gain as Tehran Says Hormuz Is Open
stories.summary: The dollar wiped out all the gains it registered since the US-Iran war began after Tehran announced that the Strait of Hormuz is now “completely open” for commercial traffic, further denting demand for haven assets.

stories.headline: Iran foreign minister says Strait of Hormuz ‘completely open’
stories.summary: Iran says 'passage for all commercial vessels through Strait of Hormuz' open during the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire.

stories.headline: Oil prices drop over 10% after Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz 'completely open'
stories.summary: Oil prices have dropped over 10% in less than two hours after the Minister for Foreign Affairs of Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz "completely open" for the remainder of the current ceasefire with the US.

stories.headline: Israeli attacks kill several over two days in Gaza despite ‘ceasefire’
stories.summary: As Gaza violence continues, activist says Israeli settlers torch vehicles, attack homes in occupied West Bank.

stories.headline: Oil tumbles after Iran pledges to open strait during Lebanon truce
stories.summary: Crude falls 10 per cent following statement from Iranian foreign minister Abbas Araghchi

stories.headline: Oil and Gas Prices Plunge After Iran Says Hormuz Strait Is Open
stories.summary: Oil and gas prices plunged early Friday after Iran said the Strait of Hormuz is now completely open to traffic. But Bloomberg's Mia Gindis says it may take some time for tanker traffic to bounce back. She reports on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: EU prepares for jet fuel sharing as supplies dwindle
stories.summary: Energy commissioner Dan Jørgensen says Europe moving towards supply crisis “very rapidly”

stories.headline: Trump says 'thank you' after Iran announces opening of Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Iran and US declare Strait of Hormuz "completely open," but Iran adds that passage is on a "coordinated route" set by Tehran.

stories.headline: ‘Waiting without answers’: Gaza mother’s fear for her three imprisoned sons
stories.summary: As Palestinians mark Prisoners' Day, Inaam al-Dahdouh waits for news of her three sons, taken by Israel two years ago.

stories.headline: Australia's most decorated living soldier released on bail over war crime charges
stories.summary: Lawyers argued that Ben Roberts-Smith wouldn't be able to defend himself properly from prison.

stories.headline: Harry and Meghan meet Bondi shooting survivors
stories.summary: On the fourth day of their Australian visit, the royal couple pay tribute to the 15 people killed in the Bondi shooting.

stories.headline: Wall Street Turns Gloomy on the Dollar as Haven Demand Fades
stories.summary: Deutsche Bank AG and Wells Fargo & Co. are among banks declaring the dollar’s war-driven haven rally is likely over as the ceasefire between the US and Iran prompts investors to seek riskier assets.

stories.headline: House votes to briefly extend warrantless surveillance law amid GOP divisions
stories.summary: Trump officials say the program is vital to national security, but skeptics — including some Republicans — have stonewalled its reauthorization without changes to protect civil liberties.

stories.summary: EU officials and the International Energy Agency have contrasting narratives over jet fuel supply. While the European commission minimises the risk, it acknowledges that the market remains “tight” and says the EU is prepared to respond appropriately.

stories.headline: Empty LNG Tankers Attempt Hormuz Crossing Into Persian Gulf
stories.summary: Two liquefied natural gas tankers appear to be attempting to enter the Persian Gulf via the Strait of Hormuz, which — if they succeed — would be a first during the Middle East energy crisis.

stories.headline: Paramount-Warner Merger Will 'Desecrate America': Kirsten Vangsness
stories.summary: Kirsten Vangsness, actress and writer, joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Markets." More than 3,000 Hollywood actors, directors and screenwriters have penned a letter opposing the $110 billion takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery by Paramount Skydance Corp., citing concerns about job losses and higher costs with fewer choices for consumers. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: House Votes to Extend Expiring FISA Surveillance Law for 10 Days
stories.summary: The Senate would need to also approve the stopgap measure that passed the House early Friday. Libertarian-leaning House Republicans had balked at a long-term extension.

stories.headline: Acting ICE Director Says He Plans to Resign in May
stories.summary: Todd Lyons said he would leave to spend more time with his family. He has spoken about a surge in threats against ICE officers, saying that he knew the reality firsthand.

stories.headline: Italy: 25 hostages freed as Naples bank gang vanishes underground
stories.summary: A group of three masked robbers held 25 people hostage during a bank heist in Naples on Thursday 16 April 2026 before escaping through a tunnel connected to the city’s sewer network, authorities said.

stories.headline: Finance ministers and top bankers raise serious concerns about Mythos AI model
stories.summary: Experts say Mythos potentially has an unprecedented ability to identify and exploit cyber-security weaknesses.

stories.headline: The Best of Watches and Wonders 2026
stories.summary: Despite an up and down year for the timepiece trade, the vibe at the industry’s biggest show was upbeat. Even goofy. Chris Rovzar reports from Geneva. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Panel Advances Trump’s Triumphal Arch, Even as Key Member Suggests Changes
stories.summary: The Commission of Fine Arts, a Trump-aligned advisory body, granted preliminary approval. But its vice chairman suggested losing statues atop the structure and other revisions before a final vote.

stories.headline: Bulgaria elections: Who’s running and what’s at stake?
stories.summary: Bulgaria has held seven national elections in the past four years because of political and social divisions.

stories.headline: Myanmar pardons over 4,000 prisoners, including deposed president
stories.summary: Myanmar pardons former leader Win Myint, while lawyer for Aung San Suu Kyi says her jail sentence has been reduced.

10-day ceasefire in Lebanon begins as Israel agrees to U.S.-backed deal
stories.summary: President Donald Trump announced the agreement, which went into force Thursday evening, as Pakistani mediators worked to extend a U.S.-Iran ceasefire and arrange new talks.

stories.headline: Oil and Gas Plunge After Iran’s Araghchi Says Hormuz Strait Open
stories.summary: Oil and European natural gas prices tumbled after Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said the Strait of Hormuz “is declared completely open,” even as a US naval blockade continues to constrain flows through the critical waterway.

stories.headline: Trump says Iran agrees to hand over ‘nuclear dust’
stories.summary: Iran has not confirmed Trump’s claim. Giving up its highly enriched uranium would be a major step toward an agreement.

stories.headline: Naples bank robbers hold 25 people hostage then vanish through tunnel
stories.summary: The armed men reportedly evaded capture by escaping through the city's sewer system.

stories.headline: Pope criticises 'tyrants' who spend billions on wars after Trump spat
stories.summary: The comments follow a high-profile spat with US President Donald Trump, who called the Pope weak on crime.

stories.headline: Turkish police detain 162 people over online posts about school shootings
stories.summary: At least 16 people were injured in a shooting at a high school on Tuesday, before another nine were killed in a separate school shooting on Wednesday.

stories.headline: Europe has 'maybe six weeks of jet fuel left', energy boss warns

stories.headline: Russia launches deadliest aerial attack in months, killing 18 in Ukraine

stories.headline: Japan pledges $10bn to help Asian countries deal with oil crisis
stories.summary: The aid is roughly equivalent to a year's worth of crude oil imports by Asean countries.

stories.headline: Stocks Rally While Oil, Dollar Drop on Hormuz Hope: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Equities climbed after Iran announced that the Strait of Hormuz is now “completely open” for commercial traffic, prompting traders to take on more risk after an extraordinary rally. Oil and the dollar tumbled.

stories.headline: Officials Investigate Bomb Threat at Home of Pope’s Brother
stories.summary: The police in suburban Chicago said they found no explosive devices. The investigation comes amid a public dispute between the pope and President Trump over the Iran war.

stories.headline: House Votes to Preserve Deportation Protections for Haitians, Rebuking Trump
stories.summary: The action was largely symbolic since the president would be all but certain to veto the bill, but the bipartisan vote reflected resistance within his own ranks on his signature issue.

stories.headline: Judge Again Halts Aboveground Construction on Trump Ballroom
stories.summary: A federal judge wrote that an exception he made for work on security features did not cover most of the construction on the larger ballroom project Trump has proposed.

stories.headline: Federal judge sets new limits on Trump ballroom construction
stories.summary: U.S. District Judge Richard Leon clarified that some national security work can continue while the overall project is halted. Trump attacked him on social media.

stories.headline: House votes to restore protections for Haitians, defying Trump
stories.summary: Ten House Republicans joined Democrats to oppose President Donald Trump on his immigration policy Thursday, voting to restore temporary protections for Haitians living in the United States.

stories.headline: UK seeks closer EU ties in volatile times - but at what cost?
stories.summary: The UK is adopting a "ruthlessly pragmatic" approach to becoming closer to its European neighbours, the UK's EU minister tells the BBC.

stories.headline: 'How does one survive?': Factory protests expose strain in India's industrial system
stories.summary: Workers in some north Indian cities have been protesting, demanding better pay and working conditions.

stories.headline: 'Unprecedented' fire at Australian oil refinery to affect nation's petrol supplies
stories.summary: The fire has deepened fears over the nation's petrol supplies amid a global crunch.

stories.headline: US eases sanctions on state-run Venezuelan banks
stories.summary: The move will make it easier for the banks to conduct transactions in dollars.

stories.headline: Trump says China has agreed not to send weapons to Iran
stories.summary: The president made the claim, which Beijing has yet to confirm, amid anger in China that its vessels could be caught up in a U.S. blockade targeting Iranian ports.

stories.headline: Magyar calls for fast handover of power in talks with Hungary's president
stories.summary: Péter Magyar, the man who ended Viktor Orbán's 16 years of continuous rule, wants the new parliament to convene in early May.

stories.headline: Spain approves plan to give around 500,000 undocumented migrants legal status
stories.summary: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez said migrants helped "build the rich, open and diverse Spain that we are and to which we aspire".

stories.headline: Bongbong Marcos fights ill-health rumours with star jumps
